trickyParts: Cross-account security group management — the Pulumi stack runs against the management account (for IAM Identity Center), but the dev box security group is in the dev account. Solved with an explicit aws.Provider that assumes a role in the dev account.
Pulumi import for brownfield adoption — the security group already exists, so the resource uses the import option and protect:true to adopt it without recreation.
No plan comments existed on issue 453 — implemented directly from issue body requirements.
deviations: Added cross-account AWS provider (dev-account) with assumeRole support, which was not explicitly in the issue spec but is required for correctness since the SG is in the dev account while the stack runs in the management account.
Added protect:true on the security group resource to prevent accidental deletion of the brownfield resource.
openQuestions: devBoxSecurityGroupId in Pulumi.prod.yaml must be set to the actual SG ID before first pulumi up.
devAccountRoleArn must be configured if running from management account credentials (optional if running with dev-account credentials directly).
Verification of SSH being blocked and SSM working requires actual AWS access — cannot be verified in this environment.
The package-lock.json was generated by npm install for the standalone Pulumi project — this is correct since aws-infra uses runtime:nodejs (ts-node), not bun.
